title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Program Brief: Sdl Boundary
summary: A script imports an incompatible pygame distribution, opens a window during import, ignores failed modules and quit events, presents twice, and never cleans up. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sdl boundary increment in owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-sdl-boundary — Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_runtime_sdl_loop_4(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-sdl-boundary-7u4ks0
# Competency: asteroids-sdl-boundary.
# Case 5079: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_sdl_boundary_7u4ks0(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_7u4ks0 = "7u4ks0-0sk4u7-5079"
    assert evidence_variant_7u4ks0
    evidence_axes_7u4ks0 = (
        "player7u4ks0",
        "seed5079",
        "input0sk4u7",
        "frame7u4ks05079",
        "state50797u4ks0",
        "playtest0sk4u75079",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_7u4ks0)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory Asteroids team handles case 1590 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Pygame is a game engine that owns every application decision.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries. Artifact under review: owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop.

options.text: The Sdl Boundary record says owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect sdl boundary evidence boundary for Sdl Boundary.

For inspect sdl boundary evidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Sdl Boundary for owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop.
The record names the assumption, applies Sdl Boundary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sdl Boundary model · Theory Sdl Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Sdl Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3209 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sdl Boundary model · Theory Sdl Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieving Sdl Boundary through the concrete owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sdl Boundary model · Theory Sdl Boundary, keep Sdl Boundary connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sdl Boundary model · Theory Sdl Boundary, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Sdl Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sdl Boundary model · Theory Sdl Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 3209 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Sdl Boundary model · Theory Sdl Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Pygame is a game engine that owns every application decision.
